summary: PR 2481 split the tail of melee/lb/lbmthp.c into a new matching object melee/lb/lb_01F8.c, adjusted GALE01 splits and symbol metadata for lb data/string ownership, moved shared lbmthp struct declarations into the public header, and made small matching/data fixes in lb_00F9, lbmthp, lbsnap, lbrefract, and lbshadow. The bot report recorded 13 new matches, including lb_01F8 text, bss, and sdata2, plus matched data gains in nearby lb units.

key_files.role: New matching translation unit containing lbMthp8001F890, lbMthp8001F928, lbMthp8001FAA0 and local bss struct lbl_804335B8_t.
Original lbmthp unit shortened at 0x8001F890; tail functions removed and minor matching fixes applied to assertions and THPVideoDecode calls.
evidence_refs: diff:lbmthp.c changes streamPlayer copy to pointer and THPVideoDecode third argument cast

key_files.role: Header gains GXStruct include, THPVideoDecode prototype correction, and struct lbl_804333E0_t definition shared by lbmthp.c and lb_01F8.c.
evidence_refs: diff:THPVideoDecode third parameter changed from s32 to void*

key_files.role: Static header no longer owns lbl_804333E0_t definition after it moved to lbmthp.h.
evidence_refs: diff:lbmthp.static.h removes GXStruct include and struct lbl_804333E0_t block

key_files.role: Updates lbmthp ranges and introduces new split entry for melee/lb/lb_01F8.c.
evidence_refs: diff:splits.txt lbmthp text end changes 0x8001FC08 to 0x8001F890

key_files.role: Refines lb/lbshadow/lbsnap/lbmthp data symbol boundaries, string labels, sizes, scopes, and data encodings.
evidence_refs: diff:symbols.txt splits lbShadow_803BA0FC and adds lbl_803BA12C
diff:symbols.txt replaces lbSnap_803BAD00 aggregate with individual string/sjis labels
diff:symbols.txt replaces str_arr_803BADB0 and str_arr_803BAE3C aggregates with individual labels

key_files.role: Adds melee/lb/lb_01F8.c as a Matching object.
Small matching/data cleanup: assert filename override, const Vec3 data, and local float initialization order/value changes.
Changed file in lb data cleanup; exact hunk content not fully loaded in excerpt.
Changed file in lb data cleanup; exact hunk content not loaded in excerpt.
Changed file in lb data cleanup; exact hunk content not loaded in excerpt.

what_changed: Created src/melee/lb/lb_01F8.c and moved three lbMthp tail functions from lbmthp.c into it: lbMthp8001F890, lbMthp8001F928, and lbMthp8001FAA0.
Updated GALE01 splits so lbmthp.c text ends at 0x8001F890 and new lb_01F8.c owns text 0x8001F890-0x8001FC08, bss 0x804335B8-0x80433658, and sdata2 0x804D7CE0-0x804D7CE8.
Added lb_01F8.c to configure.py as Object(Matching, "melee/lb/lb_01F8.c").
Changed THPVideoDecode prototype third argument from s32 to void* and updated call sites to pass/cast pointer values accordingly.
Moved struct lbl_804333E0_t from lbmthp.static.h into lbmthp.h so lb_01F8.c can extern Movieplayer with the shared type.
Replaced generated-looking float symbol use lbl_804D7CE0 in moved lb_01F8 code with inline 0.0F literals.
Adjusted lbmthp assertion code to use a pointer to Movieplayer instead of copying the whole struct before HSD_ASSERTREPORT.
Fixed an HSD_ASSERTREPORT format string in fn_8001F13C by removing an embedded newline from the literal.
Refined symbols.txt data metadata by splitting aggregate symbols into smaller string/sjis labels and adjusting lbShadow_803BA0FC size.
In lb_00F9.c, added a local __FILE__ override to "lbspdisplay.c", made two Vec3 objects const, and swapped local float initializers for far and ortho_bot.

smart_moves.text: The PR used a real split entry plus configure.py object addition instead of leaving tail functions in the old lbmthp unit, which made lb_01F8 text, bss, and sdata2 independently match.
The shared Movieplayer struct was moved from a static header into lbmthp.h when the new translation unit needed the same type, avoiding duplicate local struct definitions in the diff.
The PR corrected a function prototype to represent a pointer parameter directly and then adjusted call sites, rather than continuing to pass pointer-like values through an s32 prototype.
evidence_refs: diff:THPVideoDecode prototype changes third parameter to void*

smart_moves.text: Data progress was achieved through symbol/split metadata and const/literal adjustments, not by adding broad dummy storage in the loaded excerpt.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a contiguous tail range of a nonmatching translation unit has distinct bss/sdata2 ownership and can be matched as its own unit, consider a split update plus a new Matching object rather than forcing the whole old unit to carry the code.
evidence_refs: diff:splits.txt lbmthp range shortened and lb_01F8 ranges added

decomp_lessons.lesson: If a split exposes a type needed by multiple translation units, move the recovered struct to the shared header instead of keeping it in a static header tied to one source file.
evidence_refs: diff:lb_01F8.c uses extern struct lbl_804333E0_t Movieplayer

decomp_lessons.lesson: Prototype truth can be part of matching cleanup: once call sites show an argument is a pointer buffer, changing the prototype from integer to void* may remove casts at the better-owned call site and make remaining casts explicit.
evidence_refs: diff:lbmthp.h THPVideoDecode third parameter s32 to void*
diff:lbmthp.c THPVideoDecode casts data->unk_98 to void*

decomp_lessons.lesson: For data-section cleanup, split aggregate string-array symbols into address-sized string/sjis labels when the metadata supports smaller labels; this can improve data matching without changing C control flow.
evidence_refs: diff:symbols.txt str_arr_803BADB0 and str_arr_803BAE3C replaced by individual labels

naming_conventions.name: lb_01F8.c
naming_conventions.convention: The new file name follows the address-style lb split naming convention for a range beginning at 0x8001F890 rather than inventing a semantic module name.

naming_conventions.name: lbl_804335B8_t / lbl_804335B8
naming_conventions.convention: The PR keeps address-style names for the new local bss struct and object where semantics are only partly known, while adding field comments for obvious texture/image/width/height roles.
evidence_refs: diff:lb_01F8.c comments image ptr for tex0/tex1/tex2, width, height

naming_conventions.name: lbl_803BAD00-style data labels
naming_conventions.convention: The symbols metadata uses address-style lbl_ names for newly split data/string labels rather than speculative semantic names.
evidence_refs: diff:symbols.txt added lbl_803BAD00, lbl_803BAD1C, lbl_803BAD48, lbl_803BAD74
diff:symbols.txt added lbl_803BAE3C through lbl_803BAFCC labels

naming_conventions.name: pWidth / pHeight
naming_conventions.convention: Local pointer variables in lbMthp8001F928 use role names for width and height where the field purpose is clear.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Translation-unit split with section ownership updates
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The tail text, bss, and sdata2 ranges were separated from lbmthp.c into lb_01F8.c and then configured as a Matching object.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Inline literal restoration for small float constants
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: Moved code in lb_01F8.c uses 0.0F directly where the old lbmthp.c version referenced lbl_804D7CE0.
evidence_refs: diff:lbmthp.c removed lbl_804D7CE0 uses in deleted tail code
diff:lb_01F8.c uses 0.0F in x80/x84 and GXInitTexObjLOD calls

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Prototype correction for pointer argument matching
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: THPVideoDecode's third parameter became void*, with call sites updated to pass a buffer pointer or cast pointer-like storage.
fn_8001E910 now takes a pointer to Movieplayer for the assertion check instead of copying the full struct to a local variable.
evidence_refs: diff:lbmthp.c streamPlayer changes from struct value to pointer

assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: lb_00F9.c undefines and redefines __FILE__ as "lbspdisplay.c", apparently to match assert/report file-string ownership in that source range.
symbols.txt changed aggregate data entries into individual string/sjis labels and adjusted object sizes to improve data matching.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Report recorded matched code 66.55% (+0.03%, +1232 bytes), linked code 38.85% (+0.02%, +888 bytes), matched data 38.85% (+0.04%, +480 bytes), and linked data 33.15% (+0.01%, +168 bytes).

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: The bot listed 13 new matches, including lbMthp8001F928, lbMthp8001FAA0, lbMthp8001F890, lb_01F8 .bss, lb_01F8 .sdata2, lb_00F9 .rodata, lbshadow data/sdata, lbsnap data, lbrefract sdata, lbmthp fn_8001F13C, lbmthp .sdata2, and lbmthp .bss.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: The bot also listed apparent regressions for lbMthp8001F928, lbMthp8001FAA0, and lbMthp8001F890 under the old lbmthp unit; these correspond to functions removed from lbmthp.c and reintroduced as new 100% matches in lb_01F8.
No human review comments were included in the loaded PR slice.

text_corpus.jsonl:bot report and empty PR body
notes: The PR body is empty and no human review comments are present, so intent beyond the diff and bot report is not supported.

The loaded raw diff is truncated; exact hunks for lbsnap.static.h, lbrefract.c, and lbshadow.c are not available in the excerpt, so their file-specific details are limited to changed-file metadata and bot report outcomes.
The bot report provides matching/regression metrics, but no local build command, objdiff command, or reviewer acceptance rationale is included.

The apparent regressions for lbMthp8001F890/lbMthp8001F928/lbMthp8001FAA0 are interpreted only as old-unit report movement because the diff shows those functions removed from lbmthp.c and added to lb_01F8.c; no broader regression conclusion is claimed.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use a real TU split when a tail range becomes independently matchable
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2481 supports a candidate lesson that a tail range should be split with GALE01 splits and configure.py updates when the new unit owns distinct text/bss/sdata2 ranges and reaches matching status. Evidence: lbmthp.c shortened at 0x8001F890, lb_01F8.c added for 0x8001F890-0x8001FC08, and bot report shows lb_01F8 functions/sections at 100%.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Move proven shared structs out of static headers when a new TU needs them
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2481 supports a candidate lesson that splitting code may require moving a recovered struct from a static header to the public owning header so another source file can use the same type without duplication.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer truthful pointer prototypes over integer placeholders
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2481 supports a candidate lesson that a function prototype should use a pointer type when call sites pass a buffer pointer; THPVideoDecode changed from s32 to void* for its third parameter and call sites were updated.

source_update_candidates.title: PR 2481 introduced matching split melee/lb/lb_01F8.c
source_update_candidates.text: In PR 2481, GALE01 splits gained melee/lb/lb_01F8.c with text 0x8001F890-0x8001FC08, bss 0x804335B8-0x80433658, and sdata2 0x804D7CE0-0x804D7CE8; configure.py added it as Object(Matching, "melee/lb/lb_01F8.c").
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a path/split ownership fact supported by the PR diff and may be useful for future lookup.

source_update_candidates.title: PR 2481 shortened lbmthp.c split after moving tail functions
source_update_candidates.text: In PR 2481, melee/lb/lbmthp.c text range changed from ending at 0x8001FC08 to ending at 0x8001F890; its bss end changed from 0x80433658 to 0x804335B8 and sdata2 end from 0x804D7CE8 to 0x804D7CE0.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a concrete split-boundary update, but should be curated as path metadata rather than inferred as a general standard.

source_update_candidates.title: PR 2481 made lbmthp.h own struct lbl_804333E0_t
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2481 moved struct lbl_804333E0_t from src/melee/lb/lbmthp.static.h into src/melee/lb/lbmthp.h and added GXStruct.h include support there.
source_update_candidates.reason: This records header ownership observed in the PR without promoting a broader rule.

source_update_candidates.title: PR 2481 refined lb data symbols around 0x803BA0FC and 0x803BAD00-0x803BAFCC
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2481 changed symbols.txt data metadata by resizing lbShadow_803BA0FC, adding lbl_803BA12C, replacing lbSnap_803BAD00 with individual string/sjis labels, and replacing str_arr_803BADB0/str_arr_803BAE3C aggregates with individual lbl_ address labels.
source_update_candidates.reason: This may belong in a data-symbol ownership sheet, but should be reviewed against current symbols and map metadata before acceptance.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider documenting split-plus-config matching workflow
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2481 is evidence that a matching change may require coordinated source movement, splits.txt ranges, symbols.txt metadata, and configure.py Object(Matching) updates. Existing standards cover data sections and truthful headers; curator may decide whether a more explicit split workflow note is useful.
source_update_candidates.reason: Candidate standard only; the PR is a single example and does not by itself establish global policy.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that __FILE__ overrides are generally acceptable from this PR alone; the diff shows one override in lb_00F9.c, but no reviewer explanation or objdiff command is included.
Do not treat address-style lbl_ names as semantically meaningful; the PR mostly preserves conservative address-based naming for data labels and unknown structs.
Do not claim all lbsnap/lbrefract/lbshadow source changes from this PR because their exact hunks are not fully present in the loaded excerpt.
Do not promote the bot report's old-lbmthp regressions as true functional regressions; the loaded diff supports that the listed functions moved to lb_01F8 and matched there.
